title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jimmy, Bondel, and Bunty discuss Jimmy's recent activities. Jimmy reveals he has been searching the house for clues regarding "seven o'clock" and presents a small bottle containing a mysterious white powder, though he refuses to disclose where he found it. Bunty then brings up a nearly burned glove that Detective Batter had asked Jimmy to try on. Bondel observes that because Jimmy's right hand is bandaged, the glove—which was large—was actually worn on his left hand. Bondel concludes that Detective Batter used the distraction of the glove's size to hide the fact that the shooter was left-handed. 

Jimmy recalls that Sir Oswald Cutt is ambidextrous and notes that during a bridge game at the chimney house on the night Gary Wade died, Sir Oswald was dealing the cards left-handed. The group speculates that Sir Oswald might be connected to the "seven o'clock" mystery and the events at the Double Dragon Church, suggesting he may have orchestrated a farce to avoid suspicion. They also recall Inspector Bartel plucking ivy from the millionaire's coat, wondering if the inspector had suspected him from the start.

Meanwhile, Lord Kettlem, who is attempting to hide a wrist injury, is visited by George Lomax. After an initial attempt to avoid the visitor, Kettlem receives George. George expresses a desire to speak with Eileen (Bondel). George praises Eileen's maturity, intelligence, and ambition, eventually declaring his intention to marry her. He argues that despite their age difference, he can provide her with a respectable social position and that their union would be mutually beneficial.
